message:
feat(obligation): Tighten linearity invariant + cancel drain tests

CRDT:
- is_linear() now requires resolves <= acquires (prevents resolve-without-
  acquire violations, not just double-acquire/double-resolve)
- force_abort_repair() guards against repairing healthy terminal entries:
  skips entries that are not in conflict, are linear, and are terminal

Graded:
- Promote on_resolve debug_assert! to assert! — double-resolution is a
  logic bug that must fail fast, not silently corrupt in release builds

Lyapunov:
- Minor additions (9 lines) to stability tracking

Ledger tests:
- cancel_drain_aborts_all_region_obligations: verify all pending obligations
  in a region are leaked/aborted during drain
- cancel_drain_multi_task_region: multi-task region drain via abort
- region_isolation_during_drain: draining one region leaves other regions
  unaffected
- drain_ordering_is_deterministic: verify pending_ids_for_region returns
  monotonically increasing IDs (BTreeMap guarantees)

Symbol cancel tests:
- test_grandchild_inherits_cancellation: 3-level propagation
- test_child_cancel_does_not_propagate_upward: isolation
- test_cancel_preserves_first_reason: first-wins semantics
- test_multiple_listeners_all_notified: fan-out verification
- test_cleanup_multiple_objects_independent: per-object isolation
- test_token_serialization_roundtrip_deterministic: serde stability
- test_message_forwarding_exhausts_at_zero_hops: hop limit
- test_broadcaster_separate_tokens_independent: no token ID conflation

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -289,8 +289,13 @@ impl GradedScope {
     }
 
     /// Record a resolution (obligation resolved in this scope).
+    ///
+    /// # Panics
+    ///
+    /// Panics if called more times than `on_reserve`, which would indicate
+    /// a double-resolution bug.
     pub fn on_resolve(&mut self) {
-        debug_assert!(
+        assert!(
             self.resolved < self.reserved,
             "on_resolve called more times than on_reserve ({} >= {})",
             self.resolved,
